source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In the circuit shown in <image>, the opamps are ideal but have saturation voltages of \(\pm 10 \, V\). Assume that the initial inductor current is \(0 \, A\). The input voltage \(V_i\) is a triangular signal with peak voltages of \(\pm 2 \, V\) and a time period of \(8 \, \mu s\). Which one of the following statements is true?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: \(V_{01}\) is delayed by \(1 \, \mu s\) relative to \(V_i\), and \(V_{02}\) is a trapezoidal waveform

choice_map.A: \(V_{01}\) is delayed by \(2 \, \mu s\) relative to \(V_i\), and \(V_{02}\) is a triangular waveform.
choice_map.B: \(V_{01}\) is not delayed relative to \(V_i\), and \(V_{02}\) is a trapezoidal waveform.
choice_map.C: \(V_{01}\) is not delayed relative to \(V_i\), and \(V_{02}\) is a triangular waveform.
choice_map.D: \(V_{01}\) is delayed by \(1 \, \mu s\) relative to \(V_i\), and \(V_{02}\) is a trapezoidal waveform
